title: Understand: The Bill of Rights
description: Check your understanding of the adoption of and content within the Bill of Rights with this set of multiple-choice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
Some of the rights protected in the United States—such as free speech, a free press, freedom of religion, and the right to a jury trial—were not listed in the original Constitution. 

**They were added in 1791 with the adoption of what?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is the Bill of Rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The first ten amendments to the Constitution
A document declaring independence from England
A document that explains how to amend the Constitution
A document that describes the structure of the federal government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which Constitutional Convention delegate and primary author of the Virginia Declaration of Rights could accurately be described as one of the “fathers” of the Bill of Rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The language of the Virginia Declaration of Rights can be seen as the inspiration for which founding document?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What year was the Bill of Rights adopted?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The protections of the Bill of Rights originally applied against abuses by which level of government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which amendment has “incorporated” nearly every right in the Bill of Rights against abuses by state governments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is the technical term for taking key rights enshrined in the Bill of Rights and applying them to the states?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which national figure was not at the Constitutional Convention but advocated for the addition of a bill of rights to the Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Many Federalists argued that the U.S. Constitution did not need a bill of rights. 

**What was the reasoning behind their argument? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: States already had bills of rights in their state constitutions.
By listing out specific rights, a bill of rights might suggest that other rights weren’t protected.
The national government was a government of limited powers, so a federal bill of rights was unnecessary.
All of the necessary protections for individual rights were already in Article V.
A bill of rights would put too many limits on the federal government, making it difficult to govern.

assessment_items.question_content:
**An Anti-Federalist would likely agree with which statement?**


[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: The American people should ratify amendments that limited the powers of the national government.
A federal bill of rights was unnecessary because each state had its own bill of rights in their constitutions.
There should be an amendment that outlawed state militias as the national military should be responsible for border protection and national security.
Including a bill of rights in the U.S. Constitution would lead to federal abuse of power, as any rights not listed would not be protected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which group initially opposed amendments to the new Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
